label: TypeScript replaces JavaScript as the industry standard for modern web development
label_zh: TypeScript 取代 JavaScript，成為現代網頁開發的行業標準

timeline.title: JetBrains Makes TypeScript Features Free as Language Solidifies Status as Universal Industry Standard
timeline.title_zh: JetBrains 將 TypeScript 功能免費化，語言鞏固其普遍行業標準地位
timeline.sub_title: Following the general availability of TypeScript 6.0, major IDE updates and AI-driven design tools are removing the final barriers to total TypeScript adoption across the global software ecosystem.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 繼 TypeScript 6.0 正式發布後，主要的 IDE 更新和人工智能驅動的設計工具正在消除 TypeScript 在全球軟件生態系統中全面採用的最後障礙。
timeline.summary:
The landscape of modern web development reached a definitive milestone today, March 19, 2026, as major tooling providers and software giants synchronized their efforts to cement TypeScript as the non-negotiable industry standard. In a landmark announcement, JetBrains revealed that core TypeScript and JavaScript development features—previously locked behind its premium 'Ultimate' subscription—are now available for free in IntelliJ IDEA v2026.1. This shift acknowledges that type-safe development is no longer a specialized enterprise requirement but a fundamental necessity for all software engineers, particularly those working in full-stack and cloud-native environments.

This move by JetBrains follows the general availability release of TypeScript 6.0 on March 17, which industry analysts are describing as the 'end of the JavaScript-based era.' TypeScript 6.0 serves as a critical bridge release, introducing strict-by-default configurations and deprecating legacy module systems to prepare the ecosystem for the upcoming TypeScript 7.0. The [ref:3, 1, 2] next major version, codenamed 'Project Corsa,' will feature a complete compiler rewrite in the Go programming language, promising build speeds up to 10 times faster than current standards. By forcing [ref:4] a migration checkpoint through version 6.0, Microsoft has signaled that the industry is moving away from the performance limitations of self-hosted JavaScript compilers toward native, parallelized infrastructure.

Complementing these architectural shifts, Google introduced 'Vibe Design with Stitch' yesterday, a revamped AI-native platform that generates high-fidelity user interfaces. Crucially, the platform defaults to generating TypeScript code, reinforcing a trend where AI coding agents perform significantly better when guided by static types. Recent data indicates that LLM-generated code experiences a 94% reduction in logic errors when verified through TypeScript's type-checking compared to plain JavaScript. This 'AI preference' has become a primary driver for adoption, as organizations increasingly rely on automated tools like Cursor and GitHub Copilot to handle large-scale code generation.

Industry metrics further validate this transition. While JavaScript remains a massive presence in legacy systems, TypeScript has officially overtaken both Python and JavaScript in total contributor activity on platforms like GitHub. Major frameworks including React, Next.js, and Svelte 5 have moved to TypeScript-first scaffolding, making 'vanilla' JavaScript a legacy choice for professional projects. For the average developer in 2026, the debate over whether to use types has been settled by the market; job listings now overwhelmingly require TypeScript proficiency as a baseline skill, frequently offering a salary premium over JavaScript-only roles.

As the industry looks toward the mid-2026 launch of the Go-based compiler, the transition is being viewed as the most significant language shift in over a decade. The combination of native execution speeds, AI-augmented workflows, and now free professional-grade tooling has effectively deprecated the use of untyped JavaScript for any project of meaningful scale. Developers are being urged to audit their dependencies and adopt 'type-stripping' workflows in Node.js 25 to remain compatible with the future of the web.
timeline.summary_zh:
現代網頁開發的格局於2026年3月19日達到了決定性的里程碑，主要工具提供商和軟件巨頭同步行動，鞏固了 TypeScript 作為不容置疑的行業標準。在一次里程碑式的公告中，JetBrains 透露，其核心 TypeScript 和 JavaScript 開發功能——此前被鎖定在其高級「Ultimate」訂閱中——現在可在 IntelliJ IDEA v2026.1 中免費獲取。這一轉變認可了類型安全開發不再是專門的企業級需求，而是所有軟件工程師，特別是那些從事全棧和雲原生環境的開發者，不可或缺的基本要求。

JetBrains 的這一舉動緊隨3月17日 TypeScript 6.0 的正式發布，業界分析師稱其為「JavaScript 時代的終結」。TypeScript 6.0 作為關鍵的橋接版本，引入了嚴格預設的配置，並棄用舊的模塊系統，為即將到來的 TypeScript 7.0 做好生態系統準備。下一個主要版本，代號為「Project Corsa」，將採用 Go 語言重寫整個編譯器，預計比當前標準快10倍的構建速度。通過強制執行版本 6.0 的遷移檢查點，微軟已表明業界正從自託管 JavaScript 編譯器的性能限制轉向原生、並行化的基礎設施。[ref:3, 1, 2, 4]

為了配合這些架構轉變，Google 昨日推出了「Vibe Design with Stitch」，一個經過改進的人工智能原生平台，用於生成高保真用戶界面。至關重要的是，該平台預設生成 TypeScript 代碼，這強化了一種趨勢，即人工智能編碼代理在靜態類型指導下，其性能顯著提升。近期數據顯示，與純 JavaScript 相比，LLM 生成的代碼在通過 TypeScript 的類型檢查驗證後，邏輯錯誤減少了94%。這種「AI 偏好」已成為採用的主要驅動力，因為組織越來越依賴 Cursor 和 GitHub Copilot 等自動化工具來處理大規模代碼生成。

行業指標進一步證實了這一轉變。雖然 JavaScript 在遺留系統中仍然佔有巨大份額，但 TypeScript 在 GitHub 等平台上的總貢獻者活動量已正式超越 Python 和 JavaScript。包括 React、Next.js 和 Svelte 5 在內的主要框架已轉向 TypeScript 優先的腳手架，使得「原生」JavaScript 成為專業項目的遺留選擇。對於2026年的普通開發者而言，是否使用類型的爭論已經由市場解決；職位列表現在絕大多數要求 TypeScript 熟練度作為基本技能，且經常提供高於純 JavaScript 角色的薪酬。

隨著行業邁向 Go 編譯器預計於2026年中旬的發布，這一轉變被視為十多年來最重大的語言變革。原生執行速度、AI 增強的工作流程以及現在免費的專業級工具的結合，已經有效地使未類型化的 JavaScript 在任何有意義規模的項目中使用變得過時。開發者被敦促審查其依賴項，並在 Node.js 25 中採用「類型剝離」工作流程，以保持與 Web 未來的兼容性。

timeline.title: TypeScript 6.0 Release Marks Official Displacement of JavaScript as Primary Global Web Development Standard
timeline.title_zh: TypeScript 6.0 發布，正式取代 JavaScript 成為全球首選 Web 開發標準
timeline.sub_title: Following its rise to the top of GitHub’s contributor rankings, TypeScript has solidified its role as the primary choice for enterprise development, supported by framework defaults and native runtime integration.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 在登上 GitHub 貢獻者排行榜榜首後，TypeScript 在框架默認設置和原生運行時集成支持下，已鞏固其作為企業開發首選的地位。
timeline.summary:
The landscape of software engineering has reached a definitive turning point as of March 2026, with TypeScript officially supplanting JavaScript as the industry standard for modern web development. This transition is punctuated by the recent release of the TypeScript 6.0 Release Candidate, which serves as a strategic bridge toward a massive architectural overhaul. While JavaScript remains the underlying runtime of the web, it has effectively been relegated to a compilation target for the vast majority of professional enterprise projects.

Data from the most recent industry reports, including the 2025 GitHub Octoverse, confirms this shift. For the first time in history, TypeScript has overtaken both JavaScript and Python to become the most-used language on GitHub by monthly contributors, reaching over 2.6 million active developers. This growth, a 66% year-over-year increase, is largely attributed to the synergy between strictly typed systems and artificial intelligence. AI coding [ref:2, 4] assistants and agents have been found to produce significantly more reliable code when operating within the guardrails of a type system, with some studies suggesting that nearly 94% of LLM-generated compilation errors are caught by TypeScript’s type-checker before they reach production.

The technical barriers to entry have also dissolved over the past year. Node.js [ref:6, 3] now supports native TypeScript execution by default through type-stripping, allowing developers to run TS files directly without complex build toolchains. Furthermore, the industry is closely watching the progress of 'Project Corsa,' a ground-up rewrite of the TypeScript compiler in Go. This transition, expected to reach full stability with version 7.0 later this year, promises to deliver build speeds up to ten times faster than the current JavaScript-based engine, addressing the long-standing criticism of slow compilation times in massive codebases.

The job market has mirrored this technical evolution, with approximately 78% of all JavaScript-related job postings now listing TypeScript as a mandatory or strongly preferred skill. Major frameworks such as React, Next.js, and Vue have reached a state of 'framework-driven force,' where new projects are scaffolded in TypeScript by default. Today’s development [ref:1] of new SDKs for decentralized storage systems like NeoFS further illustrates this trend, as third-party providers now prioritize TypeScript-native tooling to satisfy the demands of the modern engineering ecosystem. As the industry moves toward more complex, AI-assisted, and distributed architectures, the safety and scalability provided by TypeScript have moved it from an optional enhancement to the fundamental bedrock of the web.
timeline.summary_zh:
隨著 2026 年 3 月的到來，軟件工程領域迎來了一個決定性的轉折點，TypeScript 正式取代 JavaScript 成為現代 Web 開發的行業標準。這一轉變以最近發布的 TypeScript 6.0 Release Candidate 為標誌，它作為向大規模架構重塑的戰略橋樑。雖然 JavaScript 仍然是 Web 的底層運行時，但對於絕大多數專業企業項目而言，它已有效地淪為編譯目標。[ref:1]
根據最新的行業報告，包括 2025 年 GitHub Octoverse 在內，數據證實了這一轉變。TypeScript 有史以來第一次在 GitHub 月度貢獻者數量上超越了 JavaScript 和 Python，擁有超過 260 萬活躍開發者。這一增長，同比增長 66%，主要歸因於嚴格類型系統與人工智能的協同作用。研究表明，AI 編碼助手和代理在類型系統的約束下運行時，能夠產生更可靠的代碼，近 94% 的 LLM 生成的編譯錯誤在 TypeScript 的類型檢查器中被捕獲，然後才進入生產環境。[ref:2, 4]
過去一年，進入技術的門檻也已大大降低。Node.js 現在透過類型剝離（type-stripping）默認支持原生 TypeScript 執行，允許開發者直接運行 TS 文件，無需複雜的構建工具鏈。此外，業界正密切關注「Project Corsa」的進展，這是 TypeScript 編譯器用 Go 語言從頭開始的重寫項目。預計在今年晚些時候 7.0 版本中達到完全穩定狀態的這一轉變，有望實現比目前基於 JavaScript 的引擎快十倍的構建速度，解決了長期以來對大型代碼庫編譯速度緩慢的批評。[ref:6, 3]
就業市場也反映了這種技術演變，大約 78% 的 JavaScript 相關職位空缺現在將 TypeScript 列為必需或強烈推薦的技能。React、Next.js 和 Vue 等主要框架已達到「框架驅動的力量」狀態，新項目默認使用 TypeScript 進行腳手架搭建。今天，諸如 NeoFS 等去中心化存儲系統的新 SDK 的開發進一步說明了這一趨勢，因為第三方提供商現在優先考慮 TypeScript 原生工具，以滿足現代工程生態系統的需求。隨著行業向更複雜、由 AI 輔助和分散式架構發展，TypeScript 提供的安全性和可擴展性已使其從可選增強功能變成了 Web 的根本基石。
